text: During a late-night briefing in Brussels, Ursula von der Leyen met OpenAI policy staff, while journalists from Reuters kept asking whether Microsoft would fund a joint safety office in Dublin before the next summit.

After leaving a panel in San Francisco, Jensen Huang joined a private dinner hosted by NVIDIA and Stanford University, where Mary Chen argued that pilot production should move from Taipei to Austin if supply risks grow.

In a memo circulated between Berlin and Warsaw, Anna Kowalska wrote that Siemens should coordinate with Airbus legal teams in Toulouse, because a delayed component review had already affected contracts negotiated by Olaf Meyer.

Although the meeting notes claimed the draft came from 'the London office,' Priya Nair clarified on a call from Singapore that SoftBank analysts and Amazon procurement managers had jointly edited the risk table.

When Satya Nadella arrived in Seattle for a strategy retreat, The New York Times reported that Microsoft and OpenAI were quietly comparing incident-response playbooks developed in Zurich after last year's audit.

At 06:40, Elena Smirnova sent a voice note from Dubai saying Boeing lawyers in Chicago had accepted revised clauses, but only if Deloitte consultants in London rechecked the compliance appendix before filing.

Minutes before the webcast started in Toronto, Carla Mendes reminded UNESCO and UNICEF teams that Rafael Gomez would present from Lisbon, while a backup line remained open to Geneva in case the studio failed.

Jack Dorsey, speaking at a fintech forum in New York, said Stripe engineers in San Francisco were testing a prototype with IBM researchers, even though internal reviewers in Boston still flagged unresolved privacy questions.

In correspondence labeled 'draft-v7-final-final', Natalia Orlova wrote from Madrid that Airbus negotiators would meet Rosatom delegates in Vienna, provided external counsel from Baker McKenzie joined by noon.
Liu Wei told ByteDance executives that a pilot recruiting drive with Beijing University should start in Shenzhen, but he later added that legal review must remain with outside counsel in Hong Kong.
While waiting in Munich airport, Tim Cook approved a schedule where Apple would brief SAP and Siemens in Berlin, then send a smaller architecture group to Prague for follow-up workshops.
'Do not publish yet,' wrote Sergey Brin to Sundar Pichai after a review at Google in Mountain View, noting that Reuters had already contacted external experts in London and Palo Alto.
After the Washington roundtable ended, Pierre Dupont said the World Bank and OECD would co-author recommendations, but only if a regional consultation in Nairobi included local legal clinics.
Anita Sharma, who had just moved from Delhi to Bangalore, told Amazon HR that onboarding materials from McKinsey were outdated and needed a rewrite before the Hyderabad launch.
At a hospital conference in Boston, Pfizer asked Dr. Michael Reed to compare lab staffing plans from Cambridge and Basel, because Novartis had announced a competing program earlier that week.
Although the first draft mentioned only Paris, John Miller confirmed Meta would split the campaign between Paris and Lyon, and that UNICEF media advisors would review every external statement.
A cross-border audit showed that Samsung teams in Seoul and Busan had sent inconsistent figures to NVIDIA in Santa Clara; Maria Ivanova was assigned to reconcile both versions before Friday.
Before dawn in Istanbul, Alexey Petrov briefed Yandex security staff and warned that leaked screenshots appeared to reference an internal dashboard also used by contractors in Moscow and Kazan.
Olga Kuznetsova told Rosatom managers that an equipment shipment routed through Novosibirsk had been delayed near Yekaterinburg, forcing emergency calls with suppliers based in Astana.
During a policy rehearsal in Geneva, Rafael Gomez read comments from the United Nations legal office, then asked UNESCO editors in Rome to adjust references before publication.
The legal appendix prepared in Amsterdam stated that IBM and Accenture would supervise migration planning in Rotterdam, while Elena Smirnova coordinated stakeholder interviews from The Hague.
At a renewable-energy workshop in Oslo, Natalia Orlova said Siemens would share turbine telemetry with Statkraft, but only after consultants from PwC completed a controls review in Bergen.
Sundar Pichai noted that Google Cloud teams in Tokyo had outperformed expectations, yet he asked Sergey Brin to review a separate incident report drafted by Deloitte in Nagoya.
In a corridor outside the conference hall in Doha, Mary Chen told Reuters that OpenAI lawyers had coordinated with the Qatar Foundation and external advisers from London throughout the night.
After a delayed train from Milan, Pierre Dupont arrived in Zurich and explained that OECD analysts would cross-check World Bank financing assumptions with UBS economists by Monday.
A transcript from Mexico City quoted Carla Mendes saying UNESCO would reopen talks with local schools, while UNICEF's regional office in Monterrey drafted contingency plans for field teams.
Tim Cook and Satya Nadella exchanged notes at an industry dinner in Los Angeles, where Apple, Microsoft, and NVIDIA discussed talent pipelines with UCLA administrators.
In a briefing packet marked confidential, Anita Sharma said Amazon's launch in Johannesburg depended on approvals from local regulators, plus technical sign-off by Accenture engineers in Cape Town.
Jensen Huang, speaking from Taipei, said NVIDIA would expand its partner lab with TSMC in Hsinchu, but deferred final budget approval to a finance committee in New York.
At the end of a marathon hearing in Brussels, Ursula von der Leyen cited reports from Reuters and the Financial Times, then asked OpenAI and Microsoft delegates to reconvene in Luxembourg next month.
